vectors.description: E10 — Liana Simple Inheritance single-key + 1-year recovery
E12 — Liana Expanding Multisig 2-of-2 + recovery key
E14 — Decaying multisig 3-of-3 to 2-of-3 with 6 distinct keys
Coldcard — representative BIP 388 export shape (2-of-3 sortedmulti)
Taproot single-leaf multi_a 2-of-3 script-path (4 distinct keys)
wsh(multi(2,...)) with two master-key fingerprints (Phase E)

negative_vectors.description: HRP that is not 'wdm' → InvalidHrp
negative_vectors.provenance: BIP 173 bech32 string with HRP `bc` (a Bitcoin segwit-v0 address); decode rejects at HRP check

negative_vectors.description: Mixed-case characters in a WDM string → MixedCase
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded `wsh(pk(@0/**))`, then uppercased the data character at position 5

negative_vectors.description: String length in reserved 94–95 char range → InvalidStringLength
negative_vectors.provenance: constructed `wdm1` + 94 `q` chars; the 94..=95 data-part length range is reserved-invalid in WDM

negative_vectors.description: Non-bech32 character 'b' in data part → InvalidChar
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded `wsh(pk(@0/**))`, then replaced the data character at position 5 with `b` (not in the bech32 alphabet)

negative_vectors.description: Two character substitutions (BCH uncorrectable) → BchUncorrectable
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded `wsh(pk(@0/**))`, then flipped 5 data characters (positions 4..=8); exceeds the v0.2 BCH t=4 correction radius

negative_vectors.description: Unsupported version byte in chunk header → UnsupportedVersion
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded a 2-byte chunk-header buffer `[0x01, 0x00]` (version=1, type=SingleString) via `encoding::encode_string`; chunk-header parse rejects the unsupported version

negative_vectors.description: Unsupported card-type byte in chunk header → UnsupportedCardType
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded a 2-byte chunk-header buffer `[0x00, 0x02]` (version=0, type=2 unknown) via `encoding::encode_string`; chunk-header parse rejects the unsupported card type

negative_vectors.description: Reserved wallet-id bits set → ReservedWalletIdBitsSet
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded chunked-header bytes with the wallet-id high nibble set (0x10 in the wid first byte); chunk-header parse rejects the reserved bits

negative_vectors.description: Chunk count = 0 → InvalidChunkCount
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded chunked-header bytes with count byte = 0 (must be 1..=32); chunk-header parse rejects

negative_vectors.description: Chunk index ≥ count → InvalidChunkIndex
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded chunked-header bytes with index=3 and count=3 (index must be < count); chunk-header parse rejects

negative_vectors.description: Chunk header bytes truncated → ChunkHeaderTruncated
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded a 1-byte chunk-header buffer `[0x00]` via `encoding::encode_string`; chunk-header parse needs at least 2 bytes (SingleString) or 7 (Chunked)

negative_vectors.description: Empty chunk list → EmptyChunkList
negative_vectors.provenance: requires lower-level API: `chunking::reassemble_chunks(&[])` rejects an empty slice with `EmptyChunkList`; `decode()` rejects `&[]` earlier with a different variant

negative_vectors.description: Single-string chunk appearing more than once → SingleStringWithMultipleChunks
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded `wsh(pk(@0/**))` to a single SingleString chunk, then submitted the same string twice; reassembly rejects

negative_vectors.description: Mixed SingleString + Chunked in one decode list → MixedChunkTypes
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded `wsh(pk(@0/**))` once with default options (SingleString) and once with `ChunkingMode::ForceChunked`, then submitted both chunks together; reassembly rejects mixed types

negative_vectors.description: Wallet-id mismatch across chunks → WalletIdMismatch
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded the C5 corpus policy twice with distinct `wallet_id_seed` overrides under `ChunkingMode::ForceChunked`, then submitted chunk 0 from encoding A together with chunk 1 from encoding B; reassembly rejects the wallet-id mismatch

negative_vectors.description: Total-chunks mismatch across chunks → TotalChunksMismatch
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised two Chunked chunks with the same wallet-id but different `count` headers (2 vs 3); reassembly rejects

negative_vectors.description: Chunk index out of range → ChunkIndexOutOfRange
negative_vectors.provenance: requires lower-level API: `Chunk::new` (bypass) + `reassemble_chunks` triggers `ChunkIndexOutOfRange`; via a WDM string, `ChunkHeader::from_bytes` rejects index>=count earlier with `InvalidChunkIndex` instead

negative_vectors.description: Duplicate chunk index in a multi-chunk set → DuplicateChunkIndex
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised two Chunked chunks with identical wallet-id, count=2, and index=0 (different fragments); reassembly rejects the duplicate index

negative_vectors.description: Missing chunk index in a multi-chunk set → MissingChunkIndex
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised two Chunked chunks claiming count=3 with indices [0, 2] (index 1 absent); reassembly rejects

negative_vectors.description: Cross-chunk integrity hash mismatch → CrossChunkHashMismatch
negative_vectors.provenance: chunked a 50-byte synthetic bytecode into a 2-chunk plan, flipped one bit in the last fragment, then re-encoded each chunk; reassembly rejects on the cross-chunk SHA-256 mismatch

negative_vectors.description: Unknown tag byte 0xC0 in bytecode → InvalidBytecode(UnknownTag)
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised bytecode `[0x00, 0xC0, ...]` (unknown tag 0xC0 at the path-declaration slot), wrapped in a SingleString chunk; bytecode parse rejects

negative_vectors.description: Bytecode truncated (only header byte) → InvalidBytecode(UnexpectedEnd)
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised a 1-byte bytecode payload `[0x00]` (header only, no path declaration), wrapped in a SingleString chunk; bytecode parse rejects with UnexpectedEnd

negative_vectors.description: LEB128 varint overflow in bytecode path component → InvalidBytecode(VarintOverflow)
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised an explicit-path declaration with 11 LEB128 continuation bytes (`[0x80;11]`) that never terminates, wrapped in a SingleString chunk; bytecode parse rejects with VarintOverflow

negative_vectors.description: Trailing bytes after template tree → InvalidBytecode(TrailingBytes)
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded `wsh(pk(@0/**))` to bytecode, appended a trailing `0xFF` byte, wrapped in a SingleString chunk; bytecode parse rejects with TrailingBytes

negative_vectors.description: Reserved bits set in bytecode header byte → InvalidBytecode(ReservedBitsSet)
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised bytecode header `0x01` (reserved bit 0 set), wrapped in a SingleString chunk; bytecode parse rejects with ReservedBitsSet before reading the path declaration

negative_vectors.description: Wrong tag at path-declaration slot → InvalidBytecode(UnexpectedTag)
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised bytecode where the path-declaration slot carries Tag::Wsh (0x05) instead of Tag::SharedPath (0x33), wrapped in a SingleString chunk; bytecode parse rejects with UnexpectedTag

negative_vectors.description: k > n in multi threshold (type-check failure) → InvalidBytecode(TypeCheckFailed)
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised bytecode for `wsh(multi(k=5, n=2, @0, @1))` (k > n), wrapped in a SingleString chunk; bytecode parse rejects with TypeCheckFailed

negative_vectors.description: Non-Wsh top-level descriptor → PolicyScopeViolation
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded `wsh(pk(@0/**))`, replaced the inner Wsh content with `[Tag::Tr, Tag::Placeholder, 0x00]` (a Tr nested inside Wsh, which v0.2 still rejects), wrapped in a SingleString chunk; decode emits PolicyScopeViolation

negative_vectors.description: Malformed policy string (no valid descriptor) → PolicyParse
negative_vectors.provenance: passed the literal string `not_a_valid_policy!!!` to `WalletPolicy::from_str`; the BIP 388 parser rejects (this fixture exercises the policy-parse layer, not the WDM-string decode pipeline)

negative_vectors.description: Bytecode larger than 1692 bytes → PolicyTooLarge
negative_vectors.provenance: requires lower-level API: `chunking::chunking_decision(1693, ChunkingMode::Auto)` rejects bytecode lengths above the 1692-byte v0.1 cap; no WDM string encodes the oversized condition

negative_vectors.description: Taproot leaf miniscript uses `sha256` (not in the Coldcard subset) → TapLeafSubsetViolation
negative_vectors.provenance: encode-side rejection; `input_strings` is empty because the policy never produces a WDM string. Construct via `"tr(@0/**,and_v(v:sha256(<32B>),pk(@1/**)))".parse::<WalletPolicy>()` followed by `to_bytecode(&EncodeOptions::default())`; the encoder rejects the leaf operator `sha256`.

negative_vectors.description: Bytecode embedding `Tag::TapTree=0x08` in leaf position → PolicyScopeViolation (multi-leaf reserved for v1+)
negative_vectors.provenance: encoded `tr(@0/**)` to bytecode, appended `Tag::TapTree=0x08`, wrapped in a SingleString chunk via `encoding::encode_string`; decode rejects multi-leaf TapTree as reserved for v1+

negative_vectors.description: Fingerprints count differs from placeholder count (2 placeholders, 1 fingerprint supplied) → FingerprintsCountMismatch
negative_vectors.provenance: encode-side rejection; `input_strings` is empty because the policy never produces a WDM string. Construct via `wsh(multi(2,@0/**,@1/**)).parse::<WalletPolicy>()` and `EncodeOptions::default().with_fingerprints(vec![Fingerprint::from([0xde,0xad,0xbe,0xef])])` (one fingerprint for two placeholders); the encoder rejects with expected=2, got=1.

negative_vectors.description: Bytecode header advertises fingerprints (bit 2 set) but the fingerprints block is missing `Tag::Fingerprints=0x35` (got Tag::Wsh=0x05) → InvalidBytecode { kind: UnexpectedTag { expected: 0x35, got: 0x05 } }
negative_vectors.provenance: synthesised bytecode `[0x04, Tag::SharedPath, 0x03, Tag::Wsh, 0x00]`: header bit 2 (fingerprints) is set, but the byte where Tag::Fingerprints (0x35) is expected carries Tag::Wsh (0x05); decode emits InvalidBytecode { kind: UnexpectedTag { expected: 0x35, got: 0x05 } }
